T0041.title: Fake Provident Fund
T0041.definition: The practice of fabricating housing provident fund (HPF) contribution records—either by forging data or creating fictitious employment relationships—to fraudulently qualify for personal credit products offered by financial institutions.
T0041.description: Fraud rings use technical means to spoof HPF app data or arrange backdated contributions through shell employment, enabling applicants to pass bank eligibility checks for consumer loans and salary-based loans. This tactic specifically targets credit products that rely on HPF data for risk assessment, allowing unqualified individuals to extract large sums. Once the fraud succeeds, the lender faces a direct bad-debt loss.

references.title: Credit Fraud: Analysis of Provident Fund Forgery Loan Fraud Techniques
